metadata.description: Programming Language Type System Feature Matrix

scoring.1: Minimal — very limited or unofficial/third-party support only
scoring.2: Basic — feature present but with significant limitations
scoring.3: Moderate — usable implementation covering common cases
scoring.4: Strong — well-integrated feature with minor gaps

features.algebraic_data_types: Sum types + product types (tagged unions, enums with data)
features.effect_system: Tracked computational effects in the type system
features.gradual_typing: Mix of static and dynamic typing
features.structural_typing: Compatibility based on structure, not name
features.flow_sensitive_typing: Type narrowing based on control flow

scoring_rationale.parametric_polymorphism: Full monomorphized generics with trait bounds, where clauses, const generics
scoring_rationale.ad_hoc_polymorphism: Trait system is reference-grade; supports associated types, default impls, supertraits
scoring_rationale.algebraic_data_types: enum with data is canonical ADT; widely used (Option, Result)
scoring_rationale.pattern_matching: Exhaustive, nested, with guards; compiler-enforced completeness
scoring_rationale.ownership_lifetime: Defines the category — borrow checker, lifetimes, move semantics
scoring_rationale.higher_kinded_types: No direct HKT; workarounds via GATs (generic associated types) since 1.65
scoring_rationale.type_inference: Strong local inference (Hindley-Milner inspired) but requires annotations on fn signatures
scoring_rationale.flow_sensitive_typing: if-let, while-let, match arms narrow types

popularity.notes: Most loved language on SO for 8 consecutive years

scoring_rationale.parametric_polymorphism: Parametric polymorphism is foundational; System F + extensions
scoring_rationale.ad_hoc_polymorphism: Type classes are the original design; multi-param, functional dependencies, associated types
scoring_rationale.algebraic_data_types: Canonical definition of ADTs in programming
scoring_rationale.pattern_matching: Exhaustive, nested, as-patterns, view patterns via extensions
scoring_rationale.dependent_types: Singletons, DataKinds, TypeFamilies approximate but not full pi-types
scoring_rationale.gadts: Full GADT support with GHC extensions
scoring_rationale.higher_kinded_types: First-class HKT; Functor/Monad hierarchy depends on it
scoring_rationale.effect_system: Monadic effects (IO monad) track side effects; not algebraic effect handlers natively
scoring_rationale.refinement_types: LiquidHaskell provides refinement types as external tool
scoring_rationale.type_inference: Full Hindley-Milner (Damas-Milner) with let-polymorphism; reference implementation

popularity.notes: Dominant in PL research; strong influence on modern language design

scoring_rationale.parametric_polymorphism: Full generics with constraints, conditional types, mapped types; very expressive
scoring_rationale.ad_hoc_polymorphism: Interfaces exist but no typeclass dispatch; relies on structural matching
scoring_rationale.algebraic_data_types: Discriminated unions via literal types; no built-in match syntax
scoring_rationale.pattern_matching: Type narrowing via typeof/instanceof/in guards; no dedicated match expression
scoring_rationale.higher_kinded_types: No native HKT; encoding via generic type aliases is limited
scoring_rationale.refinement_types: Template literal types and branded types provide limited refinement
scoring_rationale.gradual_typing: Designed for gradual adoption — any, unknown, strict mode
scoring_rationale.type_inference: Strong inference with contextual typing, control flow analysis
scoring_rationale.structural_typing: Core design principle — duck typing at the type level
scoring_rationale.flow_sensitive_typing: Best-in-class narrowing via control flow analysis, type guards, assertion functions

popularity.notes: Fastest-growing typed language; dominant in web ecosystem

scoring_rationale.parametric_polymorphism: Full generics with variance annotations, bounds, context bounds
scoring_rationale.ad_hoc_polymorphism: Implicits (Scala 2) / given-using (Scala 3) provide typeclass pattern natively
scoring_rationale.algebraic_data_types: sealed trait + case class (Scala 2), enum (Scala 3)
scoring_rationale.pattern_matching: Exhaustive match with extractors, guards, sealed hierarchies
scoring_rationale.dependent_types: Path-dependent types, match types (Scala 3), not full pi-types
scoring_rationale.gadts: Supported via sealed hierarchies with type parameters; compiler support improved in Scala 3
scoring_rationale.higher_kinded_types: First-class HKT support; type lambdas in Scala 3
scoring_rationale.refinement_types: Opaque types + compiletime ops provide limited refinement
scoring_rationale.type_inference: Good local inference, improved in Scala 3; still needs annotations for complex cases
scoring_rationale.structural_typing: Structural types via Selectable (Scala 3); compound types

popularity.notes: Strong in data engineering (Spark); Scala 3 modernized the type system

scoring_rationale.parametric_polymorphism: Reified generics (inline), variance annotations, type projections
scoring_rationale.ad_hoc_polymorphism: Interfaces with default methods; no typeclass dispatch but extension functions help
scoring_rationale.algebraic_data_types: Sealed classes/interfaces; not as ergonomic as Rust/Haskell enums
scoring_rationale.pattern_matching: when expression with smart casts; exhaustive for sealed hierarchies
scoring_rationale.type_inference: Strong local inference; nullable types well-integrated
scoring_rationale.flow_sensitive_typing: Smart casts are best-in-class — automatic narrowing after null/type checks

popularity.notes: Google's preferred language for Android; growing server-side adoption

scoring_rationale.parametric_polymorphism: Added in Go 1.18 (2022); type parameter constraints, but no variance or HKT
scoring_rationale.ad_hoc_polymorphism: Interfaces are implicit (structural); no explicit typeclass/trait dispatch
scoring_rationale.type_inference: Short variable declarations (:=); limited — no inference on function signatures
scoring_rationale.structural_typing: Core design principle — interfaces satisfied implicitly by structure

popularity.notes: Dominant in cloud infrastructure (Docker, Kubernetes)

scoring_rationale.structural_typing: Struct layout compatibility / void pointer conventions give minimal structural typing

popularity.notes: Foundation of systems programming; still #2 on TIOBE

scoring_rationale.parametric_polymorphism: comptime generics — types as first-class values at compile time; very powerful
scoring_rationale.ad_hoc_polymorphism: No trait/interface system; duck-typed generics via comptime
scoring_rationale.algebraic_data_types: Tagged unions and error sets; limited compared to Rust enums
scoring_rationale.pattern_matching: switch on tagged unions with exhaustiveness; simpler than Rust match
scoring_rationale.dependent_types: comptime provides value-dependent types; e.g., [N]u8 where N is comptime
scoring_rationale.type_inference: Type inference in local contexts; explicit types required on boundaries

popularity.notes: Growing fast in systems/game dev; backed by Zig Software Foundation

scoring_rationale.parametric_polymorphism: Full dependent types subsume parametric polymorphism
scoring_rationale.ad_hoc_polymorphism: Interfaces (Idris' typeclass equivalent) with full dependent type support
scoring_rationale.dependent_types: Full spectrum dependent types — the defining feature of Idris
scoring_rationale.higher_kinded_types: Full HKT as consequence of dependent type system
scoring_rationale.effect_system: Algebraic effect handlers built into the language (Idris 2)
scoring_rationale.ownership_lifetime: Quantitative Type Theory (QTT) in Idris 2 tracks resource usage
scoring_rationale.type_inference: Good inference but dependent types often need annotations

popularity.notes: Primary showcase for full dependent types in a practical language

scoring_rationale.parametric_polymorphism: Full parametric polymorphism with value restriction
scoring_rationale.ad_hoc_polymorphism: No typeclasses; modular implicits proposed; first-class modules partially fill the gap
scoring_rationale.algebraic_data_types: Canonical ML-style ADTs; polymorphic variants add flexibility
scoring_rationale.pattern_matching: Reference implementation — exhaustive, nested, or-patterns, as-patterns
scoring_rationale.higher_kinded_types: Functors (module-level) provide HKT; not value-level like Haskell
scoring_rationale.effect_system: OCaml 5 added multicore with algebraic effect handlers (perform/match)
scoring_rationale.type_inference: Full Hindley-Milner; handles complex cases without annotations
scoring_rationale.structural_typing: Polymorphic variants, object types, row polymorphism

popularity.notes: Used in finance (Jane Street), formal verification (Coq compiler)

scoring_rationale.parametric_polymorphism: Generics with associated types, where clauses, opaque types; some limitations vs Rust
scoring_rationale.ad_hoc_polymorphism: Protocols with associated types, protocol extensions; strong but no HKT
scoring_rationale.algebraic_data_types: Enums with associated values are excellent ADTs
scoring_rationale.pattern_matching: Exhaustive switch with value binding, where clauses, tuple/enum patterns
scoring_rationale.ownership_lifetime: Ownership manifesto partially implemented; ~Copyable, consuming/borrowing in Swift 5.9+
scoring_rationale.type_inference: Strong bidirectional inference; occasional need for explicit types in closures
scoring_rationale.flow_sensitive_typing: Optional binding (if let), guard let narrow types

popularity.notes: Primary language for Apple platforms; growing server-side adoption

scoring_rationale.parametric_polymorphism: Type erasure limits runtime generics; wildcards add complexity but reduce power
scoring_rationale.ad_hoc_polymorphism: Interfaces with default methods; no typeclass dispatch
scoring_rationale.algebraic_data_types: Sealed classes + records (Java 17+); still more verbose than ML-family
scoring_rationale.pattern_matching: Pattern matching for switch/instanceof (Java 21); record patterns, guards
scoring_rationale.type_inference: var (Java 10) for local variables only; diamond operator
scoring_rationale.flow_sensitive_typing: instanceof pattern matching narrows types; limited scope

popularity.notes: Enterprise standard; massive ecosystem; modernizing with Project Amber/Valhalla

scoring_rationale.parametric_polymorphism: Templates are very powerful (Turing-complete); concepts (C++20) add constraints
scoring_rationale.ad_hoc_polymorphism: Concepts, SFINAE, overloading provide ad-hoc polymorphism; verbose compared to traits
scoring_rationale.algebraic_data_types: std::variant + std::visit; much more verbose than native ADTs
scoring_rationale.pattern_matching: No native pattern matching; structured bindings (C++17) are minimal
scoring_rationale.ownership_lifetime: RAII, unique_ptr, move semantics; no compiler-enforced borrows
scoring_rationale.dependent_types: Non-type template parameters give very limited value-dependent types
scoring_rationale.higher_kinded_types: Template template parameters; powerful but syntax-heavy
scoring_rationale.type_inference: auto, decltype, CTAD (C++17); strong but not Hindley-Milner
scoring_rationale.structural_typing: Duck typing in templates (pre-concepts); not in the nominal type system

popularity.notes: Performance-critical domains; game engines, embedded, HPC

scoring_rationale.parametric_polymorphism: typing.Generic, TypeVar; annotations only — not enforced at runtime
scoring_rationale.ad_hoc_polymorphism: Protocol classes (PEP 544), ABC; type checker enforced only
scoring_rationale.algebraic_data_types: Union types, dataclasses; match statement (3.10) but no exhaustiveness enforcement
scoring_rationale.pattern_matching: Structural pattern matching (PEP 634, Python 3.10); no exhaustiveness
scoring_rationale.gradual_typing: PEP 484 type hints + mypy/pyright; Optional is default, very gradual
scoring_rationale.type_inference: Pyright/mypy infer locally; runtime is dynamically typed
scoring_rationale.structural_typing: Protocol (PEP 544) provides structural subtyping in type checkers
scoring_rationale.flow_sensitive_typing: Pyright narrows types via isinstance/is None checks

popularity.notes: #1 language overall; dominates data science, ML, scripting

scoring_rationale.parametric_polymorphism: Reified generics (unlike Java); constraints, covariance/contravariance
scoring_rationale.ad_hoc_polymorphism: Interfaces, default interface methods; no typeclasses but static abstract members help
scoring_rationale.algebraic_data_types: Records, discriminated unions via pattern matching; not as native as F#
scoring_rationale.pattern_matching: Comprehensive patterns (C# 8-12): type, positional, property, list, relational patterns
scoring_rationale.ownership_lifetime: ref struct, Span<T> provide limited stack-only / lifetime semantics
scoring_rationale.type_inference: var for locals; target-typed new; no inference on return types
scoring_rationale.flow_sensitive_typing: Nullable reference types (C# 8) + pattern matching narrow types

popularity.notes: Unity game engine; .NET ecosystem; rapid modernization

scoring_rationale.parametric_polymorphism: Good generics via .NET; automatic generalization; some CLR limitations
scoring_rationale.ad_hoc_polymorphism: SRTPs (statically resolved type parameters); more limited than typeclasses
scoring_rationale.algebraic_data_types: Discriminated unions are first-class and idiomatic
scoring_rationale.pattern_matching: Exhaustive, nested, active patterns provide extensible matching
scoring_rationale.type_inference: Hindley-Milner based; very strong — rarely need annotations

popularity.notes: ML on .NET; strong in finance, data processing

scoring_rationale.parametric_polymorphism: Full parametric polymorphism; constrained by lack of typeclasses
scoring_rationale.ad_hoc_polymorphism: No typeclasses; number/comparable/appendable magic constraints
scoring_rationale.algebraic_data_types: Custom types (tagged unions) are the primary abstraction
scoring_rationale.pattern_matching: Exhaustive case expressions; compiler guarantees completeness
scoring_rationale.effect_system: Commands and Subscriptions model effects; managed by runtime, not algebraic
scoring_rationale.type_inference: Full Hindley-Milner; famous for helpful error messages

popularity.notes: Pioneered 'The Elm Architecture' (TEA) adopted by Redux, etc.

scoring_rationale.parametric_polymorphism: Reified generics; constraints via extends; no variance annotations
scoring_rationale.ad_hoc_polymorphism: Abstract classes/interfaces; no typeclasses
scoring_rationale.algebraic_data_types: Sealed classes (Dart 3.0); exhaustive switch on sealed hierarchies
scoring_rationale.pattern_matching: Patterns in switch/if-case (Dart 3.0); destructuring, guards, exhaustiveness
scoring_rationale.gradual_typing: Originally optionally typed; now sound null safety; some dynamic remains
scoring_rationale.type_inference: Local inference; var, final; return types sometimes inferred
scoring_rationale.flow_sensitive_typing: Promotion via null checks, is/is! checks; very usable

popularity.notes: Flutter framework; Google-backed; strong mobile cross-platform presence

scoring_rationale.parametric_polymorphism: Parametric types with type parameters; abstract types hierarchy
scoring_rationale.ad_hoc_polymorphism: Multiple dispatch is Julia's core — function dispatch on all argument types; best-in-class
scoring_rationale.algebraic_data_types: No native sum types; can encode via abstract types + structs
scoring_rationale.pattern_matching: No native pattern matching; MLStyle.jl library provides it
scoring_rationale.gradual_typing: Optional type annotations; untyped code runs dynamically with JIT
scoring_rationale.type_inference: JIT compiler performs aggressive type inference; annotations optional
scoring_rationale.structural_typing: Duck typing at runtime; abstract type hierarchy is nominal

popularity.notes: Scientific computing; aims to solve 'two-language problem'; used in climate/physics research

scoring_rationale.parametric_polymorphism: Generics with type constraints; concepts provide type-level predicates
scoring_rationale.ad_hoc_polymorphism: Method overloading + concepts; UFCS (universal function call syntax)
scoring_rationale.algebraic_data_types: Object variants (variant + of); less ergonomic than Rust/Haskell
scoring_rationale.pattern_matching: Case statements on object variants; not as expressive as ML-family
scoring_rationale.ownership_lifetime: ORC/ARC memory management (Nim 2.0); --mm:orc; not borrow checker
scoring_rationale.dependent_types: Static[T] for compile-time values; very limited
scoring_rationale.effect_system: Effect tracking via .raises, .tags pragmas; checked exceptions style
scoring_rationale.refinement_types: range types (e.g., range[0..100]) provide basic refinement
scoring_rationale.type_inference: Local type inference; proc signatures need annotations

popularity.notes: Python-like syntax with C-like performance; compiles to C/JS

scoring_rationale.parametric_polymorphism: Full parametric polymorphism with rank-N types
scoring_rationale.ad_hoc_polymorphism: Haskell-style type classes with multi-param, functional dependencies
scoring_rationale.algebraic_data_types: Haskell-style data declarations
scoring_rationale.pattern_matching: Exhaustive, nested; very similar to Haskell
scoring_rationale.higher_kinded_types: First-class HKT; critical for its Monad/Functor ecosystem
scoring_rationale.effect_system: Row-typed effects track side effects precisely
scoring_rationale.type_inference: Strong inference; needs annotations on top-level definitions
scoring_rationale.structural_typing: Row polymorphism for records; extensible records

popularity.notes: Haskell for the browser; compiles to JavaScript; strong type safety for web

scoring_rationale.parametric_polymorphism: Generics with type parameters; no constraints/bounds (by design, simplicity)
scoring_rationale.ad_hoc_polymorphism: No typeclasses, no interfaces — intentionally minimal; use module pattern
scoring_rationale.algebraic_data_types: Custom types with constructors; idiomatic and well-integrated
scoring_rationale.pattern_matching: Exhaustive case expressions; the primary control flow mechanism
scoring_rationale.type_inference: Full Hindley-Milner; zero annotations needed
scoring_rationale.flow_sensitive_typing: No — types are immutable throughout scope

popularity.notes: Runs on BEAM (Erlang VM); designed for building reliable concurrent systems

scoring_rationale.parametric_polymorphism: Generic types with abilities (Roc's trait-like mechanism)
scoring_rationale.ad_hoc_polymorphism: Abilities (like typeclasses but simpler); limited compared to Haskell
scoring_rationale.algebraic_data_types: Tag unions as primary data modeling tool
scoring_rationale.pattern_matching: Exhaustive when-is expressions; the core control flow
scoring_rationale.effect_system: Task-based effect tracking; platform/application split manages IO
scoring_rationale.type_inference: Full Hindley-Milner; no annotations required
scoring_rationale.structural_typing: Open tag unions + records are structurally typed

popularity.notes: Spiritual successor to Elm; designed by Richard Feldman; pre-1.0

scoring_rationale.parametric_polymorphism: Dynamically typed — no static generics
scoring_rationale.ad_hoc_polymorphism: Protocols provide runtime dispatch on data type; well-designed but dynamic
scoring_rationale.pattern_matching: Best-in-class for dynamic language — function heads, case, with; pervasive idiom
scoring_rationale.gradual_typing: Typespecs for documentation; Dialyzer for success typing; not enforced
scoring_rationale.type_inference: Dialyzer does type analysis but not full static inference
scoring_rationale.structural_typing: Protocols dispatch on structure; maps are structurally matched

popularity.notes: Built on BEAM VM; Phoenix framework; strong in real-time web apps

scoring_rationale.parametric_polymorphism: Dynamically typed — no static generics
scoring_rationale.ad_hoc_polymorphism: Duck typing via respond_to?; no formal protocol/interface system
scoring_rationale.pattern_matching: Pattern matching in case/in (Ruby 3.0+); structural patterns, pin operator
scoring_rationale.gradual_typing: RBS type signatures + Steep/Sorbet type checkers; growing adoption
scoring_rationale.structural_typing: Duck typing is de facto structural typing at runtime

popularity.notes: Rails framework; strong in startups/web; RBS bringing gradual types

scoring_rationale.parametric_polymorphism: Dynamically typed; no static generics
scoring_rationale.ad_hoc_polymorphism: Multimethods + protocols; very flexible runtime dispatch, well-designed
scoring_rationale.pattern_matching: core.match library; not built-in language feature
scoring_rationale.gradual_typing: core.typed (Typed Clojure) for optional static typing; limited adoption
scoring_rationale.structural_typing: Maps/records are structurally matched; spec for runtime validation

popularity.notes: Lisp on JVM; immutable data structures; strong in data processing
